title: Testing, Red Teaming, Release, Rollback, and Production Defense · Runtime Lens: Rag Accessibility Usability Qa
summary: A release has happy-path unit tests and one demo, but no component evals, injection or tenant tests, accessibility checks, load faults, rollback, deletion, restore, or stakeholder defense. Predict the stakeholder decision, corpus and query identity, relevance assumption, failure cost, and evidence that would falsify the proposed RAG claim before opening the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the accessibility usability qa increment in production RAG release and defense dossier.…
objectives: Verify keyboard, landmarks, headings, focus, status, errors, citations, tables, text alternatives, touch, reduced motion, responsive layouts, comprehension, and escalation.
Red team poisoning, indirect injection, embedding manipulation, tenant leaks, secret extraction, unsafe output, tool effects, denial of service, deletion, and incident response.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""rag-retrieval-augmented-generation cumulative evidence-grounded retrieval portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python models and fixed fixtures only.
Native ML packages, files, models, vector stores, provider APIs, networks, credentials,
OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production effects require authorized transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RagEvidence:
    corpus_version: str
    document_id: str
    chunk_id: str
    query_id: str
    source_span: str


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RetrievalBoundary:
    tenant_id: str
    authorized: bool
    answerable: bool
    context_budget: int


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python RAG engineering portfolio ready"

content.title: Worked case pattern

Build deterministic Python evidence to gate release on component, end-to-end, security, accessibility, load, rollback, and recovery evidence; keep every status keyboard reachable, announced, readable without color, and linked to structured source evidence; then change tenant, language, date, and document version and reject stale or unauthorized evidence. Browser code uses pure bounded models and fixed fixtures only. Native Python packages, embedding or generation models, vector databases, files, networks, credentials, provider APIs, OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. A defensible solution applies Rag Accessibility Usability Qa, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to production RAG release and defense dossier.

content.code:
# Evidence: rag-worked-rag-testing-release-defense-2
# Competency: rag-worked-rag-testing-release-defense-2.
# Case 7417: a regulated document deletion request.
# Operating constraint: record the failed stage, causal trace, repair, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: replace one exact identifier with a synonym and compare lexical, dense, and hybrid recall.
def worked_rag_testing_release_defense_2(unit: bool = True, retrieval_eval: bool = True, generation_eval: bool = True, security: bool = True, accessibility: bool = True, load: bool = True, rollback: bool = True, recovery: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked2 = "worked2-2dekrow-7417"
    assert evidence_variant_worked2
    evidence_axes_worked2 = (
        "corpusworked2",
        "query2dekrow",
        "tenant7417",
        "indexworked27417",
        "prompt2dekrow7417",
        "evaluation7417worked2",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked2)) == 6
    gates = (unit, retrieval_eval, generation_eval, security, accessibility, load, rollback, recovery)
    passed = sum(1 for gate in gates if gate)
    if not unit or not retrieval_eval:
        return False, passed, "deterministic-gates"
    if not generation_eval or not security or not accessibility:
        return False, passed, "quality-safety-gates"
    if not load or not rollback or not recovery:
        return False, passed, "production-gates"
    assert passed == len(gates)
    return True, passed, "production-defense"

content.code:
# Evidence: rag-accessibility-usability-qa-oxzzfa
# Competency: rag-accessibility-usability-qa.
# Case 5603: an incident-response knowledge search.
# Operating constraint: preserve source provenance, tenant authorization, deletion, freshness, citation offsets, and untrusted-data separation.
# Changed case: move the only supporting passage to the middle of the context and rerun the position slice.
def evidence_rag_accessibility_usability_qa_oxzzfa(unit: bool = True, retrieval_eval: bool = True, generation_eval: bool = True, security: bool = True, accessibility: bool = True, load: bool = True, rollback: bool = True, recovery: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_oxzzfa = "oxzzfa-afzzxo-5603"
    assert evidence_variant_oxzzfa
    evidence_axes_oxzzfa = (
        "corpusoxzzfa",
        "queryafzzxo",
        "tenant5603",
        "indexoxzzfa5603",
        "promptafzzxo5603",
        "evaluation5603oxzzfa",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_oxzzfa)) == 6
    gates = (unit, retrieval_eval, generation_eval, security, accessibility, load, rollback, recovery)
    passed = sum(1 for gate in gates if gate)
    if not unit or not retrieval_eval:
        return False, passed, "deterministic-gates"
    if not generation_eval or not security or not accessibility:
        return False, passed, "quality-safety-gates"
    if not load or not rollback or not recovery:
        return False, passed, "production-gates"
    assert passed == len(gates)
    return True, passed, "production-defense"
